rationale: The citing paper uses Mace and Neslin (2004) to support core conceptual and empirical choices about stockpiling, not merely as a passing background mention. It cites that study as evidence on the time profile of stockpiling effects and explicitly uses it when forming expected category-level relationships (for example, storability and pull-forward effects). It also positions Mace and Neslin as prior work on variation in promotion-response components, against which the current retailer-focused net impact analysis is framed.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Mace and Neslin (2004) as prior empirical grounding for stockpiling and pre/post-promotion dip behavior, then builds a broader retailer-focused framework on top of that. Specifically, it uses those insights to motivate its stockpiling assumptions and correlate expectations while extending the analysis to net unit and net profit impact.
